You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 1bf9c461).

## Issue to Resolve

0.48.0 does not allow nordic chars åäöåæø in package-names
When upgrading from 0.47.1 to 0.48.0, ktlint complains on package names containing åæø.

## Expected Behavior
Expecting æøå to be accepted

## Observed Behavior
` Package name contains a disallowed character (cannot be auto-corrected) (package-name)`

## Steps to Reproduce
Create class `Bar` in package with name `føø` and run `mvn antrun:run@ktlint`

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.0
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Apache Maven 3.8.1
* Operating System and version: macOS Ventura 13.1

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Similar to https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1530
More problems like this are likely to occur in this release. `0.48.0` contains some rules which formerly where experimental but now have been added to the `standard` rules. Also new `experimental` naming rules have been added in `0.48.x`. Can you please run ktlint with `--experimental` once to identify which of the newly added experimental rules also break on nordic characters?
It seems like it also fails on property-, class- and function-names containing nordic characters when running with experimental rules:

`Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:property-naming)" on properties containing nordic characters.`
`Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:class-naming)`
`Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:function-naming)`
Indeed, that is not entirely unexpected. Thnx for confirming though. Sorry for the inconvenience.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/ClassNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/FunctionNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/PropertyNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/internal/RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/PackageNameRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

12→ *
13→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
14→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
15→ */
16→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
17→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
18→
19→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
20→        node: ASTNode,
21→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
22→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
23→    ) {
24→        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {

27→                ?.text

37→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
38→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
39→            ?.let {

16→
17→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
18→        node: ASTNode,

22→        if (!isTestClass && node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {

25→                ?.pathStr

28→                }

31→                    isTestClass = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
37→            ?.takeUnless {
38→                node.isFactoryMethod() ||
39→                    node.isTestMethod() ||
40→                    node.hasValidFunctionName()
41→            }?.let {
42→                val identifierOffset =

44→                        .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
45→                        ?.startOffset
46→                        ?: 1

48→            }

52→        (this.psi as KtFunction)
53→            .let { it.hasDeclaredReturnType() && it.name == it.typeReference?.text }
54→
55→    private fun ASTNode.isTestMethod() =
56→        isTestClass && hasValidTestFunctionName()
57→

59→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
60→            ?.text
61→            .orEmpty()

The repository has a utility function `regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()` that converts "A-Z" to `\p{Lu}` (all Unicode uppercase letters) and "a-z" to `\p{Ll}` (all Unicode lowercase letters).

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).

## Unreleased

### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`

Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.

API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.

Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.

--
* Clarify that API Consumers have to enable experimental rules ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
* Trim spaces in the `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`'s entries ([#1770](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1770))

### Changed

## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03

### Added

### Removed

--

### Changed

* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0` and Kotlin version to `1.8.0`.

## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15

### Indent rule

The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.

--

Up until ktlint 0.46 the Rule class provided only one life cycle hook. This "visit" hook was called in a depth-first-approach on all nodes in the file. A rule like the IndentationRule used the RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier to call this lifecycle hook for the root node only in combination with an alternative way of traversing the ASTNodes. Downside of this approach was that suppression of the rule on blocks inside a file was not possible ([#631](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/631)). More generically, this applied to all rules, applying alternative traversals of the AST.

The Rule class now offers new life cycle hooks:
* beforeFirstNode: This method is called once before the first node is visited. It can be used to initialize the state of the rule before processing of nodes starts. The ".editorconfig" properties (including overrides) are provided as parameter.
* beforeVisitChildNodes: This method is called on a node in AST before visiting its child nodes. This is repeated recursively for the child nodes resulting in a depth first traversal of the AST. This method is the equivalent of the "visit" life cycle hooks. However, note that in KtLint 0.48, the UserData of the rootnode no longer provides access to the ".editorconfig" properties. This method can be used to emit Lint Violations and to autocorrect if applicable.
* afterVisitChildNodes: This method is called on a node in AST after all its child nodes have been visited. This method can be used to emit Lint Violations and to autocorrect if applicable.
* afterLastNode: This method is called once after the last node in the AST is visited. It can be used for teardown of the state of the rule.

Optionally, a rule can stop the traversal of the remainder of the AST whenever the goal of the rule has been achieved. See KDoc on Rule class for more information.

The "visit" life cycle hook will be removed in Ktlint 0.48. In KtLint 0.47 the "visit" life cycle hook will be called *only* when hook "beforeVisitChildNodes" is not overridden. It is recommended to migrate to the new lifecycle hooks in KtLint 0.47. Please create an issue, in case you need additional assistence to implement the new life cycle hooks in your rules.


#### Ruleset providing by Custom Rule Set Provider

The KtLint engine needs a more fine-grained control on the instantiation of new Rule instances. Currently, a new instance of a rule can be created only once per file. However, when formatting files the same rule instance is reused for a second processing iteration in case a Lint violation has been autocorrected. By re-using the same rule instance, state of that rule might leak from the first to the second processing iteration.

Providers of custom rule sets have to migrate the custom rule set JAR file. The current RuleSetProvider interface which is implemented in the custom rule set is deprecated and marked for removal in KtLint 0.48. Custom rule sets using the old RuleSetProvider interface will not be run in KtLint 0.48 or above.

For now, it is advised to implement the new RuleSetProviderV2 interface without removing the old RuleSetProvider interface. In this way, KtLint 0.47 and above use the RuleSetProviderV2 interface and ignore the old RuleSetProvider interface completely. KtLint 0.46 and below only use the old RuleSetProvider interface.

Adding the new interface is straight forward, as can be seen below:

--

#### Rule set providing by API Consumer

The KtLint engine needs a more fine-grained control on the instantiation of new Rule instances. Currently, a new instance of a rule can be created only once per file. However, when formatting files the same rule instance is reused for a second processing iteration in case a Lint violation has been autocorrected. By re-using the same rule instance, state of that rule might leak from the first to the second processing iteration.

The ExperimentalParams parameter which is used to invoke "KtLint.lint" and "KtLint.format" contains a new parameter "ruleProviders" which will replace the "ruleSets" parameter in KtLint 0.48. Exactly one of those parameters should be a non-empty set. It is preferred that API consumers migrate to using "ruleProviders".

```
// Old style using "ruleSets"
KtLint.format(
    KtLint.ExperimentalParams(
--
KtLint.reloadEditorConfigFile("/some/path/to/.editorconfig")
```

#### Miscellaneous

Several methods for which it is unlikely that they are used by API consumers have been marked for removal from the public API in KtLint 0.48.0. Please create an issue in case you have a valid business case to keep such methods in the public API.

### Added

* Add `format` reporter. This reporter prints a one-line-summary of the formatting status per file. ([#621](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/621)).

--
### Fixed
- Incorrect spacing around curly braces (curly-spacing).

12→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#naming-rules
13→ *
14→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
15→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
16→ */
17→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
18→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
19→
20→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
21→        node: ASTNode,
22→        autoCorrect: Boolean,

24→    ) {

28→                ?.text
29→                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
30→                ?.let {
31→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
32→                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
33→                }
34→        }
35→
36→        node
37→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
38→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
39→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
40→            ?.let {
41→                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
42→            }
43→    }
44→
45→    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
46→        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
47→

49→        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()

12→/**
13→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
14→ */
15→public class FunctionNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:function-naming") {
16→    private var isTestClass = false
17→
18→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
19→        node: ASTNode,
20→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
21→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
22→    ) {
23→        if (!isTestClass && node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
24→            (node.psi as KtImportDirective)
25→                .importPath
26→                ?.pathStr
27→                ?.takeIf {
28→                    it.startsWith(ORG_JUNIT) || it.startsWith(ORG_TESTNG) || it.startsWith(KOTLIN_TEST)
29→                }
30→                ?.let {
31→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
32→                    isTestClass = true
33→                }
34→        }
35→
36→        node
37→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
38→            ?.takeUnless {
39→                node.isFactoryMethod() ||
40→                    node.isTestMethod() ||
41→                    node.hasValidFunctionName()
42→            }?.let {
43→                val identifierOffset =
44→                    node
45→                        .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
46→                        ?.startOffset
47→                        ?: 1
48→                emit(identifierOffset, "Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case", false)
49→            }
50→    }
51→
52→    private fun ASTNode.isFactoryMethod() =
53→        (this.psi as KtFunction)
54→            .let { it.hasDeclaredReturnType() && it.name == it.typeReference?.text }
55→
56→    private fun ASTNode.isTestMethod() =
57→        isTestClass && hasValidTestFunctionName()
58→
59→    private fun ASTNode.hasValidTestFunctionName() =
60→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
61→            ?.text
62→            .orEmpty()

70→

72→        val VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = "[a-z][A-Za-z\\d]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
73→        val VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = "(`.*`)|([a-z][A-Za-z\\d_]*)".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()

Perfect! All tests are passing. Let me create a summary of the changes made:

## Summary

I've successfully fixed the issue where ktlint 0.48.0 did not allow Nordic characters (åäöæø) in package names and other identifiers. The root cause was that the regex patterns in naming rules only accepted ASCII letters (A-Z, a-z) instead of Unicode letters.

### Changes Made:

1. **PackageNameRule.kt** (ktlint-ruleset-standard):
   - Updated the `VALID_PACKAGE_NAME_REGEXP` to use `regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()` utility function, which transforms "A-Z" to `\p{Lu}` (Unicode uppercase letters) and "a-z" to `\p{Ll}` (Unicode lowercase letters)
   - Added import for `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters`

2. **RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt** (ktlint-ruleset-experimental/internal):
   - Created a new utility file in the experimental ruleset to provide the same functionality since experimental rules don't depend on standard ruleset
   - This allows the experimental naming rules to also support Unicode letters

3. **ClassNamingRule.kt** (ktlint-ruleset-experimental):
   - Updated `VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP` to use the utility function
   - Added import for the utility function

4. **FunctionNamingRule.kt** (ktlint-ruleset-experimental):
   - Updated `VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP` and `VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP` to use the utility function
   - Added import for the utility function

5. **PropertyNamingRule.kt** (ktlint-ruleset-experimental):
   - Updated `LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP`, `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP`, and `BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP` to use the utility function
   - Added import for the utility function

### How the Fix Works:

The utility function `regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()` transforms regex patterns to use Unicode property escapes:
- Replaces "A-Z" with `\p{Lu}` (matches any uppercase Unicode letter)
- Replaces "a-z" with `\p{Ll}` (matches any lowercase Unicode letter)

This allows the rules to accept:
- Nordic characters: å, ä, ö, æ, ø
- Other accented letters: é, è, ü, ö, etc.
- Any other Unicode letters in any language

### Test Results:

All naming rule tests pass successfully:
- `PackageNameRuleTest`: ✅
- `ClassNamingRuleTest`: ✅
- `FunctionNamingRuleTest`: ✅
- `PropertyNamingRuleTest`: ✅

The fix is minimal, targeted, and doesn't break any existing functionality. It simply extends the regex patterns to support Unicode letters in addition to ASCII letters, which is the expected behavior for a linting tool that should work with Kotlin code in any language.

Perfect! All tests pass. The fix is complete and working correctly. Let me provide a final summary:

## Summary of Fix

I've successfully resolved the issue where ktlint 0.48.0 did not allow Nordic characters (åäöæø) in package names, class names, function names, and property names.

### Root Cause

The regex patterns in naming rules only accepted ASCII letters (A-Z, a-z) instead of Unicode letters, causing ktlint to reject identifiers containing Nordic characters and other non-ASCII letters.

### Solution

Updated all affected naming rules to use the `regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()` utility function, which transforms regex patterns to use Unicode property escapes:
- `"A-Z"` → `\p{Lu}` (matches any Unicode uppercase letter)
- `"a-z"` → `\p{Ll}` (matches any Unicode lowercase letter)

### Files Modified

1. **ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/PackageNameRule.kt**
   - Changed `VALID_PACKAGE_NAME_REGEXP` to use Unicode letter classes

2. **ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/ClassNamingRule.kt**
   - Changed `VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP` to use Unicode letter classes

3. **ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/FunctionNamingRule.kt**
   - Changed `VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP` and `VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP` to use Unicode letter classes

4. **ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/PropertyNamingRule.kt**
   - Changed `LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP`, `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP`, and `BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP` to use Unicode letter classes

5. **ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/internal/RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt**
   - Created new utility file to provide the regex transformation function for the experimental ruleset

### Test Results

✅ All existing tests pass:
- `PackageNameRuleTest` - All tests passing
- `ClassNamingRuleTest` - All tests passing
- `FunctionNamingRuleTest` - All tests passing  
- `PropertyNamingRuleTest` - All tests passing
- Full test suite for both rulesets - All tests passing

### Impact

- **No breaking changes**: ASCII-only identifiers continue to work as before
- **Extended functionality**: Now accepts Unicode letters from any language including:
  - Nordic characters: å, ä, ö, æ, ø
  - Other accented letters: é, è, ü, etc.
  - Any other Unicode letters

The fix is minimal, targeted, and addresses the exact issue described in the problem statement. Nordic characters (åäöæø) are now properly accepted in package names and all other identifiers checked by the naming rules.

## Unreleased

### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`

Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.

API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.

Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.

## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15

### Indent rule

The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.

--

Up until ktlint 0.46 the Rule class provided only one life cycle hook. This "visit" hook was called in a depth-first-approach on all nodes in the file. A rule like the IndentationRule used the RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier to call this lifecycle hook for the root node only in combination with an alternative way of traversing the ASTNodes. Downside of this approach was that suppression of the rule on blocks inside a file was not possible ([#631](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/631)). More generically, this applied to all rules, applying alternative traversals of the AST.

The Rule class now offers new life cycle hooks:
* beforeFirstNode: This method is called once before the first node is visited. It can be used to initialize the state of the rule before processing of nodes starts. The ".editorconfig" properties (including overrides) are provided as parameter.
* beforeVisitChildNodes: This method is called on a node in AST before visiting its child nodes. This is repeated recursively for the child nodes resulting in a depth first traversal of the AST. This method is the equivalent of the "visit" life cycle hooks. However, note that in KtLint 0.48, the UserData of the rootnode no longer provides access to the ".editorconfig" properties. This method can be used to emit Lint Violations and to autocorrect if applicable.
* afterVisitChildNodes: This method is called on a node in AST after all its child nodes have been visited. This method can be used to emit Lint Violations and to autocorrect if applicable.
* afterLastNode: This method is called once after the last node in the AST is visited. It can be used for teardown of the state of the rule.

Optionally, a rule can stop the traversal of the remainder of the AST whenever the goal of the rule has been achieved. See KDoc on Rule class for more information.

The "visit" life cycle hook will be removed in Ktlint 0.48. In KtLint 0.47 the "visit" life cycle hook will be called *only* when hook "beforeVisitChildNodes" is not overridden. It is recommended to migrate to the new lifecycle hooks in KtLint 0.47. Please create an issue, in case you need additional assistence to implement the new life cycle hooks in your rules.


#### Ruleset providing by Custom Rule Set Provider

The KtLint engine needs a more fine-grained control on the instantiation of new Rule instances. Currently, a new instance of a rule can be created only once per file. However, when formatting files the same rule instance is reused for a second processing iteration in case a Lint violation has been autocorrected. By re-using the same rule instance, state of that rule might leak from the first to the second processing iteration.

Providers of custom rule sets have to migrate the custom rule set JAR file. The current RuleSetProvider interface which is implemented in the custom rule set is deprecated and marked for removal in KtLint 0.48. Custom rule sets using the old RuleSetProvider interface will not be run in KtLint 0.48 or above.

For now, it is advised to implement the new RuleSetProviderV2 interface without removing the old RuleSetProvider interface. In this way, KtLint 0.47 and above use the RuleSetProviderV2 interface and ignore the old RuleSetProvider interface completely. KtLint 0.46 and below only use the old RuleSetProvider interface.

10→/**
11→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#naming-rules
12→ *
13→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
14→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
15→ */
16→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
17→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
18→
19→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
20→        node: ASTNode,
21→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
22→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
23→    ) {
24→        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
25→            node
26→                .findChildByType(ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION)
27→                ?.text
28→                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
37→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
38→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
39→            ?.let {
40→                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
41→            }
42→    }
43→
44→    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
45→        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
46→
47→    private fun ASTNode.isTestClass() =
48→        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()
49→
50→    private fun ASTNode.hasBackTickedIdentifier() =
51→        text.matches(BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
52→
53→    private companion object {
54→        val VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
55→        val BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("`.*`")
56→    }
57→}

28→                ?.text

 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.FUN
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.experimental.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 9→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFunction
10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective

12→/**
13→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
14→ */
15→public class FunctionNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:function-naming") {
16→    private var isTestClass = false
17→
18→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
19→        node: ASTNode,
20→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
21→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
22→    ) {
23→        if (!isTestClass && node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
24→            (node.psi as KtImportDirective)
25→                .importPath
26→                ?.pathStr
27→                ?.takeIf {
28→                    it.startsWith(ORG_JUNIT) || it.startsWith(ORG_TESTNG) || it.startsWith(KOTLIN_TEST)
29→                }
30→                ?.let {
31→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
32→                    isTestClass = true
33→                }
34→        }
35→
36→        node
37→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
38→            ?.takeUnless {
39→                node.isFactoryMethod() ||
40→                    node.isTestMethod() ||
41→                    node.hasValidFunctionName()
42→            }?.let {
43→                val identifierOffset =
44→                    node
45→                        .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
46→                        ?.startOffset
47→                        ?: 1
48→                emit(identifierOffset, "Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case", false)
49→            }
50→    }
51→
52→    private fun ASTNode.isFactoryMethod() =
53→        (this.psi as KtFunction)
54→            .let { it.hasDeclaredReturnType() && it.name == it.typeReference?.text }
55→
56→    private fun ASTNode.isTestMethod() =
57→        isTestClass && hasValidTestFunctionName()
58→
59→    private fun ASTNode.hasValidTestFunctionName() =
60→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
61→            ?.text
62→            .orEmpty()
63→            .matches(VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
64→
65→    private fun ASTNode.hasValidFunctionName() =
66→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
67→            ?.text
68→            .orEmpty()
69→            .matches(VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
70→
71→    private companion object {
72→        val VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = "[a-z][A-Za-z\\d]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
73→        val VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = "(`.*`)|([a-z][A-Za-z\\d_]*)".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
74→        private const val KOTLIN_TEST = "kotlin.test"
75→        private const val ORG_JUNIT = "org.junit"
76→        private const val ORG_TESTNG = "org.testng"
77→    }
78→}
